data.narrative:
AnySignal is a Series A-stage Space & Aerospace company that has raised $34M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private peers (Aalyria, ClearSpace, Hubble Network, Sift) valued between $274M and $1.3B, supplemented by 5 public Space & Aerospace comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($34M) as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implies $68M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 private comparables yields $400M, with ClearSpace (Series A, $400M valuation) providing direct stage alignment. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, standard for private companies. The median Space & Aerospace public P/S multiple is 59.8x across the 5 public tickers.

Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $58M (low) to $460M (high) with a median of $234M. The wide range reflects tension between stage-progression mechanics and peer multiples; stage-progression anchors conservatively to raised capital, while peer-median is weighted upward by Series B comparables. Key uncertainties include AnySignal's revenue trajectory relative to peers and whether the $400M ClearSpace valuation (identical stage) or higher-valued Series B comps better reflect market conditions for this specific subsector.
